text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled amongst endless hills, a peculiar old tree stood as a quiet guardian. Every evening after the sun dips below the horizon, creatures began to descend from its boughs. There was the fluttering Tinkerbug, the chirping Willow Ptarmigan, and the soft whispers of the Sycamore Fly.

One unusually calm evening, a toddler named Lily ventured towards the old tree. She loved exploring its rustling branches. As she reached out her chubby hand to touch a particularly gnarled limb, a soft glow emanated from a hollow at the base—a nest!

With giggles sparkling in her eyes, she peered inside. Nestled among crumpled leaves were baby squirrels, each wearing tiny mittens that barely covered their little paws. They were asleep, lulled into slumber by a melody that only trees could make.

Lily listened intently and sang along. The squirrel babies woke up, chattering happily.
